docs.title: Open science for sport and health
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch connects scientific research, reproducible software and technological innovation in a single open platform.

Explore products Research areas Publications

docs.title: One platform, one source of information
docs.text:
• Scientific software

    Open and reproducible tools for data extraction, physiological monitoring, performance analysis and health research.

  • Integrated project records

    Every product brings together its GitHub repository, documentation, latest release and Zenodo DOI in one place.

  • Research output

    Scientific publications are synchronized independently and presented without duplicating software records.

  • Automatic updates

    The platform refreshes its information from GitHub, Zenodo and the University of Murcia research profile.

docs.title: From development to scientific dissemination
docs.text:
A new GitHub release, MkDocs documentation update or Zenodo record is automatically incorporated into the corresponding product card.

View unified products

docs.title: Research areas
docs.text:
• Wearable technology and validation

    Extraction, synchronization and validation of inertial, positioning and physiological data.

  • Physiological monitoring

    Longitudinal analysis of heart-rate variability, blood pressure, recovery and individual responses.

  • Exercise and health

    Scientific tools for exercise oncology, wellbeing monitoring and health-related research.

  • Performance analysis

    Applied analytics for football, rugby, refereeing and team-sport performance.

docs.title: About SportsLabResearch
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch is an independent research initiative focused on the development of scientific software, hardware and digital technologies for Sports Science.

The project was created to establish a unified ecosystem where research software, educational resources, hardware developments and scientific documentation share the same quality standards, development methodology and visual identity.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: Develop robust, reliable and reproducible scientific software following international research software engineering standards.

docs.title: Vision
docs.text: To become an international reference in research software for Sport Sciences through innovation, scientific quality and open science.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
• Scientific Excellence
  • Reproducible Research
  • Open Science
  • FAIR Principles
  • Software Quality
  • Continuous Validation
  • Long-Term Sustainability

docs.title: Research Software
docs.text: Development of scientific applications for research and professional practice.

docs.title: Artificial Intelligence
docs.text: Machine learning, computer vision and intelligent decision support systems.

docs.title: Wearable Technologies
docs.text: Heart rate variability, physiological monitoring, GPS, IMU and sports sensors.

docs.title: Sports Analytics
docs.text: Performance analysis, data science and predictive analytics.

docs.title: Arduino & Embedded Systems
docs.text: Educational and research platforms based on open hardware.

docs.title: Scientific Documentation
docs.text: Professional documentation using MkDocs and GitHub.

docs.title: Ecosystem
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch integrates:

  • Scientific Software
  • Hardware Projects
  • Educational Resources
  • Research Frameworks
  • Publications
  • Datasets
  • Technical Documentation

docs.title: Development Philosophy
docs.text:
Every project developed under SportsLabResearch follows the same workflow:

  1. Scientific idea
  2. Software design
  3. Development
  4. Scientific validation
  5. Documentation
  6. Publication
  7. Continuous improvement

docs.title: Commitment
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch promotes transparent, reproducible and sustainable scientific software designed to support researchers, lecturers and professionals worldwide.

Research Software engineered for the future of Sport Science.

docs.title: Recommended practice
docs.text: Cite the DOI of the exact version used in the analysis. Also report the version number or Git commit in the methods section.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: SportsLabResearch welcomes collaborations with researchers, universities, research centres, sports organisations, healthcare institutions and technology companies.

docs.text: SportsLabResearch promotes education through open scientific software, reproducible methodologies and practical learning resources.

docs.text:
Educational material supporting undergraduate, postgraduate and professional training.

Main topics include:

  • Heart Rate Variability
  • Blood Pressure Assessment
  • Data Science
  • Artificial Intelligence

docs.title: SportsLabResearch Framework
docs.text: The SportsLabResearch Framework defines the common methodology followed by every software and hardware project developed within the ecosystem.

docs.title: Phase 1 â€” Research
docs.text:
• Problem identification
  • Literature review
  • Scientific objectives
  • Study design

docs.title: Phase 2 â€” Design
docs.text:
• Software architecture
  • Data model
  • User workflow
  • Interface design

docs.title: Phase 3 â€” Development
docs.text:
• Modular programming
  • Version control
  • Code quality
  • Documentation

docs.title: Phase 4 â€” Validation
docs.text:
Every project must include scientific validation whenever applicable.

Validation may include:

  • Reliability
  • Accuracy
  • Precision
  • Agreement analysis
  • Statistical analysis
  • Benchmark testing

docs.title: Phase 5 â€” Documentation
docs.text:
Each project includes:

  • README
  • User Guide
  • Technical Documentation
  • Changelog
  • License
  • Citation file

docs.title: Phase 6 â€” Publication
docs.text:
Projects are disseminated through:

  • GitHub
  • GitHub Pages
  • Zenodo
  • Scientific Journals
  • Conference Communications

docs.title: Phase 7 â€” Maintenance
docs.text:
• Continuous improvement
  • Bug fixing
  • New releases
  • Scientific updates

docs.title: Core Values
docs.text:
• Scientific Excellence
  • Transparency
  • Reproducibility
  • Open Science

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Windows, macOS or Linux
  • Python 3.11 or later
  • Git

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/SportsLabResearch/SportsLabResearch-BreastCancer-Wellbeing-Analyzer.git
cd SportsLabResearch-BreastCancer-Wellbeing-Analyzer

docs.title: Install dependencies
docs.text:
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

On Windows PowerShell, py can be used instead of python:

py -m pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Verify the project
docs.text: py -m py_compile main.py

docs.title: Build this documentation
docs.text:
py -m pip install mkdocs-material
py -m mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000/.

docs.title: About SportsLabResearch
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch is an open scientific platform focused on the development of reproducible software, scientific methodologies and technological solutions for sport and health sciences.

The platform integrates scientific research, software development, documentation and knowledge transfer into a unified ecosystem that supports researchers, practitioners and educators.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: To develop open scientific software and reproducible methodologies that facilitate research, teaching and professional practice in sport and health sciences.

docs.title: Vision
docs.text: To become an international reference in open scientific software for sport and health sciences through innovation, scientific quality and collaboration.

docs.title: Core Values
docs.text:
• Scientific excellence
  • Reproducibility
  • Open science
  • Innovation
  • Collaboration
  • Knowledge transfer

docs.title: What We Do
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch develops:

  • Scientific software
  • Research frameworks
  • Data analysis tools
  • Scientific documentation
  • Educational resources
  • Open research materials

docs.title: Research Areas
docs.text:
• Sport Performance
  • Wearable Technology
  • Physiological Monitoring
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Data Science
  • Exercise and Health

docs.title: Scientific Ecosystem
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch integrates:

  • Research
  • Software
  • Documentation
  • Publications
  • Education
  • Scientific Services

All products are developed following a common philosophy of reproducibility, transparency and continuous improvement.

docs.title: Research Software Portfolio
docs.text:
Open, reproducible and scientifically validated software developed by SportsLabResearch.

🏉 Rugby 7 Referee Spatial Analysis

GNSS-based spatial and locomotor analysis of Rugby Sevens referees.

Repository Documentation DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21426713 🫀 BloodPressure Analyzer

Scientific software for automated blood-pressure analysis.

Repository Documentation Releases DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21273738 📡 WIMU DataExtractor

Automated extraction and preprocessing of WIMU datasets.

Repository Documentation DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21344653 💓 HRV-Longitudinal-Analyzer

Longitudinal analysis of heart-rate variability.

Repository Documentation Releases DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21273737

docs.text:
Each product is presented as a single unified record combining GitHub, documentation, releases and Zenodo.

docs.title: SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor
docs.text:
Open-source extractor for WIMU .QUL and .QUI files with native Excel export.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo 

<div class="slr-product-meta" markdown>
**Latest release:** v1.0.2  
**Release date:** 14/07/2026

**Language:** Python  
**License:** MIT
</div>

Automatically generated from GitHub and Zenodo.

docs.title: SportsLabResearch-BloodPressure-Analyzer
docs.text:
Open-source research software for resting blood pressure analysis, longitudinal monitoring and automated clinical reporting based on ESH 2023 recommendations.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo

Latest release: v1.0.0 Release date: 16/07/2026 DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21395622 Language: Python License: NOASSERTION

docs.title: SportsLabResearch-BreastCancer-Wellbeing-Analyzer
docs.text:
Scientific software for multidimensional assessment and longitudinal monitoring of wellbeing in women with breast cancer. Integrates patient-reported outcomes, blood pressure, SpO₂ and clinical indicators to generate automated dashboards and scientific reports. Adaptable to other clinical populations.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo

Latest release: v0.1.0 Release date: 20/07/2026 DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21459319 Language: Python License: MIT

docs.title: SportsLabResearch-Rugby7-Referee-Physical-Fatigue-Analyzer
docs.text:
Open-source scientific software for the analysis of external load, internal load and physical fatigue in Rugby Sevens referees.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo

Latest release: v0.1.0-alpha Release date: 20/07/2026 DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21451657 Language: HTML License: NOASSERTION

docs.title: SportsLabResearch-rugby7-referee-spatial-analysis
docs.text:
Open-source software for the spatial analysis of Rugby Sevens referees using GNSS data.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo

Latest release: v1.0.0 Release date: 18/07/2026 DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21426713 Language: Python License: MIT

docs.title: HRV-Longitudinal-Analyzer
docs.text:
A professional cross-platform application for longitudinal Heart Rate Variability (HRV) analysis, featuring Kubios data import, automated scientific reporting, advanced statistical analysis, participant management, and interactive dashboards for research, sports science, and clinical monitoring.

GitHub Documentation Zenodo

Latest release: v1.4.7 Release date: 09/07/2026 DOI: Not available Language: Python License: NOASSERTION

docs.title: Scientific Profiles
docs.text:
• University of Murcia profile
  • Research source

This page is generated automatically. Manual changes may be overwritten during the next update.

docs.title: 1. Prepare the data
docs.text:
Place a Google Forms export in the project input location. Supported formats include:

  • .xlsx
  • .xls
  • .csv
  • .txt

docs.title: 2. Run the application
docs.text:
From the project root:

py main.py

docs.title: 3. Select the source
docs.text: The application detects compatible questionnaire files and selects a valid source according to the project rules.

docs.title: 4. Review the outputs
docs.text:
Generated results are stored in the project output/results folders. Keep original questionnaire exports unchanged and work on copies when necessary.

Warning

Do not upload identifiable participant data to a public repository.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text: The project is designed to support clear scientific outputs without modifying the original questionnaire data.

docs.title: Expected outputs
docs.text:
• cleaned Excel datasets;
  • descriptive summaries;
  • longitudinal participant summaries;
  • tables and figures;
  • Word reports when enabled by the analysis workflow.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text: Results should be interpreted together with the study protocol, the data dictionary and the clinical context. Missing values, implausible measurements and changes in questionnaire structure must be reviewed before analysis.

docs.title: Quality checklist
docs.text:
• Confirm the participant identifier.
  • Confirm the analysis period.
  • Check the number of records.
  • Review missing and duplicated observations.
  • Confirm units and scales.
  • Record the software version used.

docs.title: Research
docs.text: SportsLabResearch connects scientific questions with reproducible software, validated methods and practical applications.

docs.title: Wearable Technology and Validation
docs.text:
Extraction, synchronization and validation of data from inertial sensors, positioning systems and physiological devices.

Related products: WIMU DataExtractor and validation tools.

docs.title: Physiological Monitoring
docs.text:
Longitudinal analysis of heart-rate variability, blood pressure, recovery and individual physiological responses.

Related products: HRV Longitudinal Analyzer and BloodPressure Analyzer.

docs.title: Exercise and Health
docs.text:
Monitoring of wellbeing, fatigue, stress, pain and physical responses in health and clinical populations, including women with breast cancer.

Related product: BreastCancer Wellbeing Analyzer.

docs.title: Sport Performance Analysis
docs.text:
Analysis of competition, external load, technical-tactical behaviour and performance indicators in team sports.

Related products: FIFA World Cup analyzers and sport-specific research tools.

docs.title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Science
docs.text: Automated processing, interpretable models, reproducible analysis and scientific reporting applied to sport and health data.

docs.title: Scientific Frameworks
docs.text:
• Intelligent Football Performance Ecosystem (IFPE)
  • Reproducible Scientific Software
  • Wearable Data Validation

docs.title: Research Workflow
docs.text: Research question → Data → Software → Validation → Publication → Knowledge transfer

docs.title: Collaboration
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch collaborates with universities, research centres, healthcare institutions, sports organisations and technology companies.

Contact Scientific Services

docs.title: Services
docs.text: SportsLabResearch provides scientific and technological services that support research, education and professional practice in sport and health sciences.

docs.title: Scientific Software Development
docs.text:
We design and develop scientific software tailored to research and professional needs.

Areas include:

  • Data acquisition
  • Data processing
  • Scientific reporting
  • Automation
  • Research workflows

docs.title: Data Analysis
docs.text:
Support for scientific data analysis including:

  • Statistical analysis
  • Longitudinal monitoring
  • Data visualisation
  • Automated reporting
  • Decision support

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Development and validation of scientific methodologies and technological systems.

Examples include:

  • Wearable technology validation
  • Device comparison
  • Concordance analysis
  • Reliability studies
  • Reproducibility assessment

docs.title: Artificial Intelligence
docs.text:
Application of Artificial Intelligence to support scientific research.

Current areas include:

  • Scientific assistants
  • Automated workflows
  • Data interpretation
  • Research support tools

docs.title: Scientific Consulting
docs.text:
Collaboration with universities, research centres, sports organisations and healthcare institutions.

Services include:

  • Research design
  • Methodological support
  • Software implementation
  • Scientific collaboration

docs.title: Education and Training
docs.text:
Training activities designed for researchers, students and professionals.

Examples include:

  • Workshops
  • Scientific software training
  • Research methodology
  • Technology in sport and health

docs.title: Collaboration
docs.text: SportsLabResearch welcomes national and international collaborations in research, software development and technology transfer.

docs.title: Software
docs.text:

docs.title: Scientific Standards
docs.text: Every project developed under SportsLabResearch follows common scientific and software engineering standards to ensure quality, reproducibility and long-term sustainability.

docs.title: Research Standards
docs.text:
• Evidence-based development
  • Scientific methodology
  • Transparent reporting
  • Reproducible research
  • Ethical research practices

docs.title: Software Standards
docs.text:
Every project includes:

  • Modular architecture
  • Version control (Git)
  • Professional documentation
  • CHANGELOG
  • LICENSE
  • CITATION.cff
  • Continuous maintenance

docs.title: Validation Standards
docs.text:
Whenever applicable, projects should report:

  • Validity
  • Reliability
  • Accuracy
  • Precision
  • Agreement
  • Benchmark comparison
  • Statistical evaluation

docs.title: Documentation Standards
docs.text:
Each repository includes:

  • README
  • User Guide
  • Technical Documentation
  • API Documentation (when applicable)
  • Examples
  • Release Notes

docs.title: Open Science Standards
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch promotes:

  • Open-source software
  • FAIR principles
  • Reproducible workflows
  • Transparent development
  • Open documentation

docs.title: Development Standards
docs.text:
The recommended workflow is:

Research

â†“

Design

â†“

Development

â†“

Validation

â†“

Documentation

â†“

Publication

â†“

docs.title: Quality Checklist
docs.text:
Every project should satisfy the following checklist before a public release:

Item Required README âœ… Documentation âœ… License âœ… CHANGELOG âœ… Citation File âœ… Version Number âœ… Validation âœ… Examples âœ… GitHub Repository âœ…

Quality is not an option; it is the foundation of every SportsLabResearch project.

docs.title: Data sources
docs.text: The source connector searches for questionnaire exports in the configured project folders and, when enabled, in synchronized Google Drive locations.

docs.title: Recommended workflow
docs.text:
1. Export or synchronize the Google Forms responses.
  2. Confirm that the file is Excel, CSV or TXT.
  3. Run the application from the repository root.
  4. Check the selected source and number of detected records.
  5. Review generated tables and reports.
  6. Archive each validated analysis with its date and software version.

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text:
For every analysis, retain:

  • the software release or Git commit;
  • the original data export;
  • the analysis date;
  • the configuration used;
  • the generated outputs.

docs.title: Data protection
docs.text: Use coded participant identifiers. Store identifiable information outside the public repository and follow the applicable ethics approval and institutional data-protection procedures.

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: The application works with the variables already defined in the project questionnaire. It does not require changing the existing Google Form.

docs.title: Main domains
docs.text: Domain Examples Participant identification coded participant identifier, date and session Wellbeing mood, stress, fatigue, pain and perceived energy Recovery sleep and perceived recovery indicators Physiological monitoring SpO₂ and blood pressure when present in the source Context treatment, session or other questionnaire-specific fields

docs.title: Variable naming
docs.text:
Column names are cleaned when data are loaded, but the original questionnaire wording should remain documented in the study data dictionary.

Note

The exact set of variables depends on the current Google Forms questionnaire and the columns present in each export.
